detailed_analysis: ToolStatusTracker is a Layer-2 use case (architecture §4) that owns per-tool available/unavailable/disabled state and logs transitions (US-11, NFR-12). It honours the Dependency Rule: it imports only agentic_tool_spec (a Layer-3 port) and config.expand_path (the documented D-2 escape hatch), references no concrete tool name, and reads config generically via dict keys. Structurally the file is sound at the class level (224 lines, 1 class, 9 methods — well under the God-Class thresholds). The one real structural defect is _probe_tool_roots (lines 139-196): ~50-line body with cyclomatic complexity well above 10 and a 4-fold-repeated unavailable/partial-availability return block — a Long Function (M2) carrying a DRY violation. The most consequential issue is a spec deviation: US-11 AC-6 mandates an ERROR-level log line when a root exists but is unreadable (permission denied / OSError), distinct from the WARN used for a merely-missing root; _log_status_transition (lines 198-223) emits only logging.info / logging.warning and never logging.error, so the unreadable-root case is logged at the wrong severity. Security surface is clean: no secrets, no injection, no unsafe deserialization. Note that NFR-15 secret-policy is not this module's concern (egress boundaries are parse/render/export/import), so its absence here is not a deviation.

findings.problem: Function body is ~50 non-blank lines (project limit 40, AGENTS.md §3) and its branch count (for + ~16 if/except/return guards) puts cyclomatic complexity around 17, well past the >10 threshold (Zhang 2023, McCabe). It conflates four concerns: kind-enablement filtering, config-key presence, on-disk reachability probing, and partial-availability status aggregation.

The identical 'build reason -> short-circuit unavailable when not partial -> else remember first_reason -> continue' block is hand-copied four times within _probe_tool_roots (config-key-missing, path-not-configured, path-missing, OSError branches). Each copy is a place a future edit can drift; this duplication is the proximate cause of CQ-01's length and complexity.

findings.variant: Primitive Obsession (untyped config dict + stringly-typed status)
findings.evidence:
self.config: dict[str, Any]
...
new_status[tool_name] = "disabled"
...
return self._status.get(tool_name) == "available"
findings.problem: The three statuses 'available' / 'unavailable' / 'disabled' — a closed, domain-defined set (US-11, glossary) — are passed around as bare string literals across get(), is_available(), refresh(), _probe_tool_roots(), and _log_status_transition() instead of an enum, and config is a raw dict[str, Any] indexed by stringly-typed keys. A typo in any literal (e.g. 'unavaliable') would fail silently rather than at type-check time. This is a maintainability/fail-fast smell, not a correctness defect today.

The constructor is a use-case entry boundary (AGENTS.md §8: validate at boundaries) but stores config and agentic_tools without any invariant check (e.g. non-None, expected key shape). A None or malformed config surfaces later as an AttributeError deep inside refresh()/_probe_tool_roots rather than as an explicit, traceable error at the boundary. Low blast radius because callers (Syncer) construct it from a validated config, hence MINOR.

deviations.description: AC-6 requires that when a root exists but is UNREADABLE (permission denied / OSError), the transition is logged at ERROR level, distinct from the WARN used for a merely-missing root (story table line 50: 'ERROR if unreadable'). The unreadable case is detected at lines 180-188 (the iterdir() OSError branch) and folded into the same reason tuple as path-missing, but _log_status_transition (lines 198-223) only ever calls logging.info (when prev is None) or logging.warning (when prev is set) for the 'unavailable' status — it never calls logging.error. An unreadable root therefore logs at WARN/INFO, not ERROR, contradicting AC-6.

NFR-14 mandates Clean Code: 'small cohesive functions'. _probe_tool_roots (lines 139-196, ~50 lines, cyclomatic complexity ~17, with a 4-fold duplicated branch block) exceeds the project's 40-line limit and the >10 complexity threshold, so the module does not fully meet the small-cohesive-functions and minimal-duplication clauses of NFR-14.

verdict_summary: No CRITICAL or security issues, but one MAJOR Long-Function/complexity defect (_probe_tool_roots) plus a US-11 AC-6 deviation (unreadable root not logged at ERROR) cap the score at 0.5.
